reforms.description: The Belgian government announced a major corporate tax reform aimed at restoring the competitive position of Belgium by reducing the corporate tax rate from 40.17% to 33.99%. This reform is expected to enhance legal and tax liability certainty, thereby making Belgium a more attractive destination for foreign direct investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering corporate tax rates is expected to attract foreign investment and enhance competitiveness, which can lead to increased economic activity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the corporate tax system.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in 2002 a major corporate tax reform aimed at restoring the competitive position of Belgium.

reforms.description: The Belgian government plans to stimulate the employment of scientific researchers through specific tax measures starting in 2003, aiming to increase the number of researchers in the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of researchers is expected to enhance innovation capacity and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it targets the employment of researchers, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: As from 2003 the employment of scientific researchers will be stimulated by specific tax measures.

reforms.description: The Belgian government introduced training vouchers in Wallonia and Flanders to provide flexible training subsidies for employers with up to 50 employees, aimed at enhancing the skills of the workforce to meet employer needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing workforce skills is expected to improve productivity and innovation capacity in small firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The training voucher program has been successfully implemented in Wallonia and Flanders.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant as it directly supports skill development in the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: The government is trying to stimulate the adaptation of skills to the needs of employers not only in schools, but also in the enterprise, by means of training vouchers.

reforms.description: The Rosetta programme was introduced to obligate firms with more than 50 employees to hire a quota of young job seekers, facilitating their transition from school to work and providing them with on-the-job training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating the transition of young job seekers into the workforce is expected to enhance human capital and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Rosetta programme has been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is significant as it addresses youth unemployment and skill development.
reforms.source_quote: The Rosetta programme, introduced in April 2000, obliges firms with more than 50 employees to recruit into their labour force a quota of at least 3 per cent of youngsters eligible for a 'First Job Agreement'.

reforms.description: The Belgian government is implementing measures to improve SMEs' access to bank credits and private equity, including tax exemptions for profits and operational subsidies for business angel networks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to finance for SMEs is expected to foster innovation and growth in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures are significant as they directly support the financial viability of SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The supply of risk capital to SMEs, especially in the case of start-ups, is considered insufficient by the government.
